compiled_state.title: validate.sh: hardcoded Python/Make targets instead of configured project commands — false failures in non-Python projects
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

validate.sh calls make syntax-check, make lint-ruff, and make lint-mypy directly, ignoring the project's dso-config.conf command configuration (commands.test, commands.lint, commands.format_check). Non-Python projects (Next.js, TypeScript) get false-positive failures.

## Expected Behavior

validate.sh should resolve project commands via read-config.sh, matching the pattern used by dso:fix-bug and other skills.

## Actual Behavior

Three false-positive failures per validation run in non-Python projects:
- make syntax-check: No rule to make target → syntax: FAIL
- make lint-ruff: fails → ruff: FAIL  
- make lint-mypy: fails → mypy: FAIL

The actual equivalents (npm run lint, npm run ts:check) pass cleanly.

## Proposed Fix

Have validate.sh resolve project commands via read-config.sh or fall back gracefully when Make targets don't exist.

## Reproduction

Run validate.sh in any non-Python project using npm/yarn commands.

## Severity

P2 — three false-positive failures per validation run, causing unnecessary triage in /dso:debug-everything.

comments.body: Investigated: validate.sh correctly reads all command config via read-config.sh (CMD_SYNTAX_CHECK, CMD_FORMAT_CHECK, CMD_LINT_RUFF, CMD_LINT_MYPY). Make targets are fallback defaults only. Bug appears based on misreading header comment examples vs actual execution logic. No code fix needed — closing as invalid.
comments.author: Test
